title: Tetraria fragilis
taxonomy: Tetraria fragilis is classified as a species within the genus Tetraria and the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. The genus Tetraria is a group of plants that are native to Africa, particularly in the southern regions. The family Cyperaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots that includes numerous species of sedges, rushes, and other related plants. Tetraria fragilis is specifically a small to medium-sized aquatic plant that thrives in wet environments. It is often found growing in shallow waters and along the edges of rivers, streams, and ponds.
